skills.name: solid-core
skills.title: SOLID Core Operating Context
skills.description: Shared SOLID philosophy: change management, scope control, truthfulness, and change-impact framing.
skills.use_when: Load before all other SOLID skills.

skills.name: solid-review
skills.title: SOLID Review Orchestrator
skills.description: Coordinates SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, and DIP for non-trivial code generation, review, and refactoring.
skills.use_when: Use for any change that can affect consumers, contracts, behaviour, architecture, tests, or future extension.

skills.name: solid-srp
skills.title: Single Responsibility Principle Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews responsibility boundaries from statement to system level using the subject-changing AND Test.
skills.use_when: Use first in SOLID review when code may mix jobs, side effects, inputs, outputs, or responsibilities.

skills.name: solid-ocp
skills.title: Open Closed Principle Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews whether change should modify the original abstraction or extend it based on consumer impact and completeness.
skills.use_when: Use after SRP to decide whether behaviour belongs in existing code or should be introduced through extension/composition.

skills.name: solid-lsp
skills.title: Liskov Substitution Principle Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews whether substitutes preserve trust, behaviour, contracts, side effects, exceptions, and consumer expectations.
skills.use_when: Use when one implementation, test double, adapter, union type, generic, API version, or component claims to stand in for another.

skills.name: solid-isp
skills.title: Interface Segregation Principle Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews whether consumers depend only on coherent contracts they need, without bloated contracts or meaningless fragments.
skills.use_when: Use for interfaces, props, DTOs, request/response objects, config, service contracts, modules, packages, APIs, and event schemas.

skills.name: solid-dip
skills.title: Dependency Inversion Principle Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews whether high-level code depends on contracts rather than concrete implementation choices.
skills.use_when: Use when code creates, imports, configures, or depends on services, SDKs, clients, loggers, repositories, providers, packages, or infrastructure.

skills.name: solid-refactor
skills.title: SOLID Refactor Playbook
skills.description: Provides pragmatic, scope-controlled refactoring guidance to improve SOLID compliance without rewriting the world.
skills.use_when: Use when a review finds a SOLID problem and the user needs the smallest safe improvement or refactor plan.

skills.name: solid-techdebt
skills.title: SOLID Technical Debt Handler
skills.description: Defines when SOLID compromise can be accepted, classified, recorded, and kept visible.
skills.use_when: Use when a SOLID issue is real but cannot be fixed safely within current scope, delivery constraints, or ownership boundaries.

skills.name: solid-pattern-library
skills.title: SOLID Pattern Library
skills.description: Guides comparison and application of known design patterns and recognition of anti-patterns without architecture cosplay.
skills.use_when: Use when a known pattern may solve a problem space, or when comparing design options and avoiding pattern abuse.
